synthesis_text: Individuals should consider getting tested for COVID-19 if they exhibit symptoms of the illness, as testing is essential for confirming infection [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00079]. This includes respiratory symptoms, which should prompt screening to rule out co-infections [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00196]. Additionally, if someone has been in contact with a confirmed case of COVID-19, they should consider testing five days after the exposure [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Children showing symptoms should also seek medical care and consider testing [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00021]. Fully vaccinated individuals with symptoms should contact their doctor regarding testing [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00013].

Individuals should consider getting tested for COVID-19 if they have symptoms of the illness, as testing is essential for those who believe they may be infected [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Additionally, testing is recommended five days after coming into contact with the virus [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Children showing symptoms should also seek medical care and consider testing [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. People with respiratory symptoms should be screened for COVID-19 to rule out co-infections [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023].

Individuals should consider getting tested for COVID-19 if they exhibit symptoms of the illness, as testing is essential for confirming infection [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00000]. This includes contacting a doctor if fully vaccinated and showing symptoms [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00013]. Children with symptoms should also seek medical care and consider testing [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00021]. Overall, testing is crucial for anyone who believes they may be infected [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00012].
